title: Quiz: Quantum Boltzmann Machine Fundamentals
description: Test your understanding of quantum Boltzmann machine concepts

questions.question: What is the quantum Gibbs state for a Hamiltonian H at inverse temperature β?
questions.explanation: The quantum Gibbs state is ρ = exp(-βH)/Z where Z = Tr[exp(-βH)] is the partition function. This is the quantum generalization of the classical Boltzmann distribution.

questions.question: What is the role of the transverse field term Γσˣ in a QBM?

options.text: It introduces quantum fluctuations and superposition
It defines the coupling between qubits

questions.explanation: The transverse field Γσˣ introduces quantum fluctuations by mixing computational basis states, enabling superposition and quantum tunneling between classical configurations.

questions.question: What happens to the QBM Gibbs state when Γ → 0?

options.text: It becomes a classical Boltzmann distribution

questions.explanation: When Γ → 0, the QBM reduces to a classical Ising model, and the quantum Gibbs state becomes diagonal in the computational basis, recovering the classical Boltzmann distribution.

questions.question: What is quantum coherence in the context of QBM Gibbs states?

options.text: The diagonal elements of the density matrix
The off-diagonal elements of the density matrix

questions.explanation: Quantum coherence refers to the off-diagonal elements of the density matrix in the computational basis. These arise from quantum superposition and are absent in classical thermal states.

questions.question: At infinite temperature (β → 0), the quantum Gibbs state becomes a uniform mixture over all states.
questions.explanation: True. As β → 0 (T → ∞), exp(-βH) → I (identity), so ρ = I/Tr[I] = I/d, which is the maximally mixed state with equal probability for all configurations.

questions.question: How is the free energy F related to the partition function Z?
questions.explanation: The free energy is F = -T log(Z) = -(1/β) log(Z). This fundamental relation connects thermodynamic quantities to the partition function.

questions.question: What method can be used to prepare quantum Gibbs states on a quantum computer?
questions.explanation: Imaginary time evolution exp(-βH)|ψ⟩ can prepare Gibbs states, as it exponentially suppresses high-energy components. Various quantum algorithms implement this or approximate it.

questions.question: Which factors affect the 'quantumness' of a QBM Gibbs state? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Quantum effects depend on (a) transverse field Γ which induces superposition, (b) temperature T where low T preserves coherence, and (d) coupling J which affects the energy landscape. The number of qubits affects system size but not intrinsic quantumness.
